title: Bayesian inference in non-Gaussian time series with global-local shrinkage process priors
Bayesian inference in non-Gaussian time series with global-local shrinkage process priors
Bayesian inference in non-Gaussian time series with global-local shrinkage process priors
text:
Taxonomy of state-space models

Linear Gaussian

\[
\begin{aligned}
\v y_{t} &=
\v C_t\v\theta_{t}+\v\varepsilon_{t}\qquad
&\v\varepsilon_{t}\overset{\mathrm{iid}}{\sim}N(\v 0,\v\Sigma_{\varepsilon,t}) \\
\v\theta_{t}
&=\v A_t \v\theta_{t-1}+\boldsymbol{\nu}_{t}\qquad
&\v\nu_{t}\overset{\mathrm{iid}}{\sim}N(\v 0,\v\Sigma_{\nu,t})
\end{aligned}
\]

Non-linear Gaussian (additive) models

\[
    \begin{aligned}
    \v y_{t} &=
    \v c_t(\v\theta_{t}) + \v\varepsilon_{t}\qquad
    &\v\varepsilon_{t}\overset{\mathrm{iid}}{\sim}N(\v 0,\v\Sigma_{\varepsilon,t}) \\
    \v\theta_{t}
    &=\v a_t(\v\theta_{t-1})+\boldsymbol{\nu}_{t}\qquad
    &\v\nu_{t}\overset{\mathrm{iid}}{\sim}N(\v 0,\v\Sigma_{\nu,t})
    \end{aligned}
\]

Non-Gaussian - general distributions

\[
    \begin{aligned}
    \v y_{t} &\sim p_t(\v y_{t} \vert \v\theta_t) \\
    \v\theta_{t} &\sim q_t(\v\theta_{t} \vert \v\theta_{t-1})
    \end{aligned}
\]

Bayesian inference in state-space models

Filtering posterior (instantaneous) \(p(\boldsymbol{z}_{t}\vert y_{1:t})\)



Smoothing posterior (retrospective) \(p(\boldsymbol{z}_{t}\vert y_{1:T})\)



Joint smoothing posterior \(p(\boldsymbol{z}_{1:T}\vert y_{1:T})\)

title: Bayesian inference in non-Gaussian time series with global-local shrinkage process priors
text:
Global-local shrinkage priors - regression [1, 2]

Linear Gaussian regression with horseshoe prior \[
\begin{aligned}
y_i &=  \v x_i^\top\v\beta + \varepsilon_i,\quad \varepsilon_i \overset{\mathrm{iid}}{\sim}N(0,\sigma^2_\varepsilon) \\
\beta_j &\overset{\mathrm{ind}}{\sim} N(0,\blue{\tau^2}\red{\lambda^2_j}) \\
\tau &\sim C^+(0,1) \\
\lambda_j &\overset{\mathrm{iid}}{\sim} C^+(0,1)
\end{aligned}
\]
Posterior mean (orthogonal \(\v x\)) equals shrunken ML estimator \[
\tilde{\beta}_j = (1-\kappa_j)\hat\beta_j
\]

title: Bayesian inference in non-Gaussian time series with global-local shrinkage process priors
section: Dynamic global-local shrinkage priors for time series
text:
Dynamic global-local shrinkage priors for time series



Local level model - dynamic shrinkage process (DSP) prior \[
\begin{aligned}
y_t & = \mu_t + \varepsilon_t \hspace{4.3cm} \varepsilon_t \overset{\mathrm{iid}}{\sim} N(0,\sigma^2_\varepsilon) \\
\mu_t &= \mu_{t-1} + \nu_t, \hspace{3.0cm} \nu_t \overset{\mathrm{ind}}{\sim} N \big(0,\blue{\tau^2}\red{\exp(h_t)}\big) \\
h_t &= \rho h_{t-1}  + \eta_t,\hspace{2.5cm} \eta_t \overset{\mathrm{iid}}{\sim}Z(\alpha,\beta,0,1) \\
\tau &\sim C^+(0,1)
\end{aligned}
\]





Global variance \(\blue{\tau^2}\)
Local variance \(\red{\lambda^2_t=\exp(h_t)}\)





DSP = horseshoe when \(\rho=0\): \[
h_t \sim Z(\alpha,\beta,0,1) \Longleftrightarrow \lambda_t = \exp(h_t/2) \sim C^+(0,1)
\]

title: Bayesian inference in non-Gaussian time series with global-local shrinkage process priors
text:
Logistic-Beta distribution (Fisher’s \(Z\))



\(X\sim \mathrm{Beta}(\alpha,\beta)\) then \[Z := \log\Big(\frac{X}{1-X}\Big) \sim Z(\alpha,\beta,0,1)\]
Location-scale: \[\mu + \sigma Z  \sim  Z(\alpha,\beta,\mu,\sigma)\]
Heavy tails - log density decays linearly.



Mean-variance mixture of Gaussians [3] \[
\begin{aligned}
Z \vert \xi &\sim N \big(\xi^{-1}(\alpha-\beta)/2,\xi^{-1}\big)  \\
\xi &\sim \mathrm{PolyaGamma}(\alpha+\beta,1)
\end{aligned}
\]

title: Bayesian inference in non-Gaussian time series with global-local shrinkage process priors
text:
Multi-seasonal ARMA with DSP prior [4]

Seasonal AR with DSP evolution \[
\phi_{p,t}(L)\Phi_{P,t}(L^{s})(y_{t}-\mu_t) = \varepsilon_{t},\quad\varepsilon_{t}\overset{\mathrm{iid}}{\sim}N(0,\sigma_{t}^{2})
\]
SAR as nonlinear Gaussian regression \[
y_t=\v z_t^{\top}\tilde{\v \phi}+\varepsilon_t
\] \(\tilde{\boldsymbol{\phi}}=(\phi_1,\Phi_1,{\color{red}{-\phi_{1}}\color{red}{\Phi_{1}}})^{\top}\) and \(\boldsymbol{z}_{t}=(x_{t-1},x_{t-s},x_{t-(1+s)})^{\top}\)
Extensions


 Multi-seasonal (daily, weekly, yearly cycle)


 ARMA and Exact likelihood


 Stochastic volatility with DSP priors


 Multi-seasonal forecasting


 Multi-seasonal VAR

title: Bayesian inference in non-Gaussian time series with global-local shrinkage process priors
text:
Gaussian approximation filters



Forward-filtering-backward sampling (FFBS) for linear Gaussian models [6, 7]

Run Kalman filter \(p(\v\theta_t\vert y_{1:t})\) forward for \(t=1,2,\ldots,T\)
Sample \(p(\v\theta_{t}\vert \v\theta_{t+1}, y_{1:T})\) backward for \(t=T-1,T-2,\ldots,1\)






Extended Kalman filter

Linearize measurement/transition models
Apply standard Kalman filter

Unscented Kalman filter

Propagate sigma point approx of prior/posterior

Prior linearization filter

Linearize by statistical linear regression

title: Bayesian inference in non-Gaussian time series with global-local shrinkage process priors
text:
Grouping for speed and Gaussianity

Need Gaussian approximations at every time step \(t\)
The prior \(\v\theta_t \sim N(\v\theta_{t\vert t-1}, \Omega_{t \vert t-1} )\) helps a lot.
No Bernstein-von Mises effect to make the likelihood contribution at time \(t\) more Gaussian.
Group observations: \(\v y_g = (y_t,y_{t-1},\ldots,y_{t-G+1})^\top\) share the same state/parameter \(\v\theta_g\):

Subsampling MCMC/HMC [8]
Filtering [Särkää]
Macroeconomic modeling [9]

Two views:

interpolation for algorithmic performance
genuine model change: different time scales for observations and parameters.

[1] Carvalho CM, Polson NG, Scott JG. The horseshoe estimator for sparse signals. Biometrika. 2010;97(2):465–480.

[4] Fagerberg G, Villani M, Kohn R. Time-varying multi-seasonal AR models. Journal of Computational and Graphical Statistics. 2026;(forthcoming):1–20.


[5] Jones MC, Faddy M. A skew extension of the t-distribution, with applications. Journal of the Royal Statistical Society Series B: Statistical Methodology. 2003;65(1):159–174.


[6] Carter CK, Kohn R. On Gibbs sampling for state space models. Biometrika. 1994;81(3):541–553.


[7] Frühwirth-Schnatter S. Data augmentation and dynamic linear models. Journal of time series analysis. 1994;15(2):183–202.


[8] Salomone R, Quiroz M, Kohn R, Villani M, Tran M-N. Spectral subsampling MCMC for stationary time series. In: International conference on machine learning. PMLR; 2020. p. 8449–8458.


[9] Celani A, Pedini L. Moderate time-varying parameter VARs. Örebro University, School of Business; 2025. Report No.: 2025:16. https://ideas.repec.org/p/hhs/oruesi/2025_016.html
